comments.author: @JohnnyTravel68
comments.text: You left out some very important information. Onassis played a massive role in the romance's timing and their subsequent 1956 wedding. As a major investor in Monaco's struggling casinos and tourism sector, Onassis strongly advised the Prince to marry a glamorous American film star to revitalize the country's economy. Aristotle Onassis transformed Monaco from a sleepy, post-war principality into a glamorous billionaires' playground by seizing control of the Société des Bains de Mer (SBM) in the 1950s. He directed his vast shipping wealth into the Monte Carlo casino, luxury hotels, and yacht infrastructure

replies.author: @MaryBond-y9s
replies.text: Should have never believed what you read in the national enquirer

replies.author: @user-fhgu28kl32
replies.text: ​@AtlasPlanetOfficialYes it is absolutely true. Thanks to Aristotelis Onassis Monaco managed to come to another level

replies.author: @MichelleHerbert-r1v
replies.text: @AtlasPlanetOfficial Johnny is 100% correct. Today's Monaco is Onassis' legacy, not the Grimaldis. When Ranier saw that Onassis was outshining him, he had him expelled from Monaco. This is so important in this story, something you have completely failed to mention.

replies.author: @luigicarlopucci4588
replies.text: ​@JohnnyTravel68i totally agree

replies.author: @JohnnyTravel68
replies.text: @AtlasPlanetOfficial But those two facts are most important as to why Monaco is what it is today. Without Onassis it would still be a struggling dump. 3 extra minutes would not have been a stretch. Without that vital information, this clip fails unfortunately.

replies.author: @AtlasPlanetOfficial
replies.text: We appreciate you adding this historical insight. Monaco's story has many fascinating layers, and it's impossible to cover everything in a single documentary. Thanks for contributing to the discussion!
